title: Are we fucked — $135 short on rent, sell the gun, two-week grind plan
html:
Are we fucked — $135 short on rent, sell the gun, two-week grind plan

Source: 2025-11-28 Google Doc. The financial-brink doc — 2 months into Austin, Yahir is $135 short on rent and grinds out a real-time scramble plan in writing.

TL;DR

  • The numbers: $790 in checking, $915 rent due, $135 gap. December even worse — out of town first week + 20-25, holiday slowdown bottlenecks the personal training income.
  • The fix: a $135 check from "that well" comes through to cover this month. Sells gun for $200 (after first estimating $250) for buffer. Plans to grind a temp job for 2 weeks back in Austin to get to next month's rent.
  • Sequenced bridge-income plan: sell gun → minimal spend during travel → temp job 2 weeks in Austin → start anew January. "This plan is 100% contingent on getting that second job that I can grind for about 2 weeks."
  • The signature pivot: even mid-financial-panic, doc closes in full manifesto mode — "We're gonna fucking make it. This is really, really making God enjoy us... I can see the life I want. I can taste it. I have the courage to speak it. It's gonna happen." The McGregor template applied to rent.
  • The ironic awareness: "This is the ultimate irony moment wow" — Yahir naming the absurdity of having to sell a firearm to make rent in the same month he's trying to grow Rankett.

Pattern observed

This doc is the rawest evidence in the corpus of the no runway constraint Yahir signed up for in October. Two months in, the "soft death / clean slate" framing meets concrete cash-flow brutality. The "make God enjoy us" framing turns suffering into spectator-sport — a move borrowed from the Connor McGregor / latent-potential-parable concept stack.

Notably, asset-liquidation appears here for the first time as a tactic (selling the gun) — adds to the bridge income toolbox alongside personal training, gym overnight shifts, mom co-sign, and dad's RV offer.

The doc is short but high-density: real-time problem-solving, identity reaffirmation, McGregor recital, and zero outside help requested. Solo grind mode.

Key quotes

  • "I am $135 short… Okay, I should be receiving a $135 check from that well, which will put me right there. This is the ultimate irony moment wow."
  • "We're gonna fucking make it... I can taste it. I have the courage to speak it. It's gonna happen. It will happen. I'm gonna do it. I'm gonna bend reality into my will. Fuck!"

Open questions

  • The gun — what kind, where bought, sold to whom? (Single mention; doesn't recur.)
  • Did the 2-week temp job materialize? (Need to check Dec/Jan thoughts-and-things docs.)
  • "That well" — informal name for some payment source (CIEE refund? a side job invoice?). Unclear.
